question_text: At sea level, atmospheric pressure is approximately 101,000 Pa. On a mountain 5 km high, atmospheric pressure is approximately 54,000 Pa. Why is the pressure lower on the mountain?
correct_answer: There is less air (fewer air particles) above the mountain, so there are fewer particle collisions, resulting in lower pressure.
explanation: Atmospheric pressure is caused by the weight of air above pressing down. On a mountain, there is less air above you, so fewer particles are colliding with surfaces, creating lower pressure.
